text:
Once upon a time there was a little girl who loved picking wild apples from the tree in her backyard. Every day she would climb up, pick an impressive pile, and bring them back to her grandmother.

One autumn morning, while gathering apples, she found a shiny, golden seed pod. Intrigued, she cracked one open, discovering a tiny bird inside.

"My goodness," the little bird said. "You're really kind!"

It turned out the little bird could sing beautifully. They spoke every night over the radio.

"Why don't you sing for us sometime?"

The bird thought for a moment and sang a beautiful, melancholy song about remembering happier times.

The girl listened every night, enchanted by the bird's voice.

Once, when the bird wasn't feeling well, she sang a special song for her.

The bird flew out and sung a big, cheerful melody, healing itself.

"I'm grateful," the girl said.

So every year on Halloween, they sing that song together on Halloween
